heading: Running Federated Queries
body:
You can use Atlas Data Federation to query and analyze a unified view of data in your
Atlas cluster, S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket or Azure Blob Storage container, HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) URL, and
online archives. For federated queries, your federated database instance storage
[Storage Configuration](config-adf) must contain the settings that define:

- Your S3 (Simple Storage Service) or Azure (Microsoft Azure), Atlas, Online Archive, and HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol) stores.

  > **Note:** Atlas Data Federation doesn't support federated queries across cloud providers.
  > Therefore, you can't run federated queries against data stored on
  > AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) buckets and Azure Blob Storage containers. Regardless of the
  > cloud provider backing your Atlas cluster, you can run
  > federated queries against data on your Atlas cluster and
  > AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket or Azure Blob Storage container simultaneously.

- Federated Database Instances with virtual collections that map to your S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket or
  Azure Blob Storage, Atlas cluster, Online Archive, and HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol)
  stores.

You can create or update your federated database instance [Storage Configuration](config-adf) using the Atlas UI **Visual Editor** or
the **JSON Editor**, Atlas Data Federation [view](adf-cli) commands, and Atlas Data Federation [API](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/group/endpoint-data-federation)
endpoints. To learn more about federated database instance storage configuration, see
[Storage Configuration](config-adf).

When you connect to your
federated database instance and run federated queries, Data Federation combines data from your
Atlas cluster, S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket or Azure Blob Storage container, and HTTP (HyperText Transport Protocol)
URL (Uniform Resource Locator)s in virtual databases and collections and returns a union of data
in the results.
